Computing Science Teacher, Mr. Valdes MSc

Interactive QTS Assessment Evidence Portfolio

This main page supports the Assessment Only route to QTS interview. It brings together the required evidence: an anonymised lesson plan approach, video evidence of practice, supporting resources, and a detailed subject knowledge audit.

What this page accomplishes: it organises evidence into a navigable portfolio, demonstrates planning and assessment across the lesson tracker, makes resources and subject knowledge examples visible, and helps structure interview preparation around the Teachers’ Standards, safeguarding, adaptive teaching and professional development.

Interview RequirementsTracker & PlansVideo EvidenceResourcesSubject AuditAI PlanningStandards

Interview Process and Evidence Requirements

Before the interview

  • Lesson plan: anonymised plan showing teaching approach.
  • Video of practice: short recording of classroom teaching.
  • Supporting resources: pupil work, displays, CPD, planning tools and subject resources.
  • Subject knowledge audit: detailed Computing subject knowledge evidence.

How to use this portfolio

Use the tracker filters to show planned lessons and open individual lesson plans. Use the resources and audit sections to evidence subject knowledge, CPD and professional judgement. Use the standards map to prepare concise STAR-style interview examples.

    Subject Knowledge Audit: Computing

    Examples below make subject knowledge visible and accessible for interview discussion.

    AI-Supported Planning Evidence

    Eduaide screenshots demonstrate the use of AI to support lesson planning, questioning, rubrics, assessment, direct instruction and collaboration. AI supports planning efficiency; professional judgement remains teacher-led.

    Outstanding-Level Evidence Map

    Quality of Education

    • Intent: sequenced Computing curriculum across digital literacy, systems, cybersecurity, programming and evaluation.
    • Implementation: explicit teaching, retrieval, modelling, adaptive support, formative assessment and feedback.
    • Impact: tracker evidence, assessment summaries, lesson plans and reflection.

    Teachers’ Standards

    • High expectations and classroom culture.
    • Good progress and outcomes.
    • Secure subject and curriculum knowledge.
    • Adaptive teaching and inclusive practice.
    • Assessment, behaviour, safeguarding and professional conduct.
    Interview preparation prompts
      Computing Science Teacher, Mr. Valdes MSc · Anonymised evidence portfolio

      Evidence Requirement

      Subject Knowledge Audit: Computing & Computer Science KS2–KS5

      This interactive audit demonstrates secure and developing subject knowledge from Year 13 down to Year 5. It links curriculum content, teaching evidence, pupil impact and CPD priorities, supporting interview discussion around subject knowledge, reflective practice, and readiness for Assessment Only QTS.

      CoverageYear 13 → Year 5
      DomainsSystems, programming, data, networks, safety, ethics
      EvidenceTracker lessons, lesson plans, video, resources, CPD
      PurposeShows accuracy, progression and improvement planning

      Reflection on Subject Knowledge

      I maintain and review my subject knowledge through curriculum planning, assessment outcomes, exam-board materials, AI-supported planning tools, professional reading and reflective lesson evaluation.

      • Accuracy: I check explanations against specifications, mark schemes and trusted subject resources.
      • Progression: I sequence knowledge from digital literacy in KS2/KS3 to technical theory, programming and evaluation in KS4/KS5.
      • Responsiveness: I use assessment evidence and lesson reflections to revisit misconceptions and adapt teaching.
      • Professional development: I use CSNewbs, Craig’n’Dave, Teach Cambridge, Save My Exams, NotebookLM, Diffit and Eduaide to strengthen planning and subject knowledge.

      Outstanding-Level Portfolio

      Fully Interactive Ofsted Evidence Map

      This map links subject knowledge audit entries to actual lessons from the tracker, clickable lesson-plan evidence, video evidence, supporting resources, AI planning evidence, Teachers’ Standards and Ofsted evidence areas.

      Use this section during interview to move quickly from a claim about practice to concrete evidence: subject knowledge → linked lessons → lesson plan → resources/video → impact and CPD.

      These resources are visible, accessible and linked to planning, CPD, subject knowledge development and adaptive teaching.
